title: The 4:07 Event
description: Party watches Brasston's synchronized teatime. Character beat, no plot advance.

steps.text: At exactly 4:07 pm Brasston Mean Time, every citizen of Brasston put down what they were holding, picked up a teacup from the nearest designated shelf, and took a single synchronized sip.

lines.speaker: Saboteur
lines.text: At the same time. Across the entire town.

lines.speaker: Alchemist
lines.text: The Schedule is very thorough. Page 144 of the civic appendix, I'd assume.

lines.speaker: Steampriest
lines.text: Some of them don't even like tea.

lines.speaker: Steampriest
lines.text: That one over there. Her face, during the sip. Exactly the face my mother made when the parish handed out that herbal mint that tasted like a pond.

lines.speaker: Steampriest
lines.text: She's done this every day for decades. She still makes that face.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: I could write a song about that.

lines.speaker: Busker
lines.text: Three stanzas. One of them a pond metaphor.

steps.text: The clock tower struck 4:08. Every citizen returned their cup to the shelf, picked up what they had been holding, and resumed the work of the day as if nothing had happened. Because, in the Schedule, nothing had.
